themes: the price of a whole skin

variables.name: water
variables.description: Skins of clean water Sef carries through the burning city. Starts at 2. Refilled (+1, capped 3) at a clean seep; consumed (-1) on hard heat crossings. Gates survival of every heat leg: a dry throat at a crossing is death. The clean escape needs at least one skin at the last crossing.

variables.name: goods
variables.description: Tradeable weight Sef has secured, culminating in the water-writ that frees the crew. Starts at 0; +1 for each act leg he advances through cleanly (salvage, trade, the writ). Gates which deals open at the Caravanserai and the finale; goods==3 is the full haul plus the prize, required for the single best ending.

variables.name: marked
variables.description: Whether the fire-wardens have Sef's face as a blood-drawer or fire-setter. False until a violent or brazen act sets it true; never falls. Governs whether he crosses the Cinder Gate and runs the Ember Road clean or hunted; the best ending requires it stay false.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=100 beats='Establish the runner Sef, a fixer for a small crew, at the rim of Ashmar, a market city burning slow for nine days; the fire never wholly ends here, it moves quarter to quarter and the trade goes on beneath it; state the cold job plainly, get in, buy the water-writ that frees the crew from the cistern-lords, and get everyone out before the wind turns the fire inward.'>>

choices.label: Cross the cinder line into the city.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=96 beats='The outer wall district; heat like a hand on the face, ash falling as grey snow, the reek of scorched cloth and tar; establish the three landmarks a runner steers by, the Cinder Gate that tolls every crossing, the Cistern that hoards the last clean water, the Caravanserai where the writ is sold; state the one hard law, the fire-wardens hunt anyone who draws blood or sets a fire inside the wall.'>>

choices.label: Take the measure of the burning streets.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='First fork, and an honest one; the smoke ahead thickens and a runner with sense could still turn back with his skin whole, leaving the crew to find another fixer or no fixer at all; or he goes in, toward the Gate, and takes Ashmar on its own terms; make the pull to walk away real and adult, not a throwaway.'>>

choices.label: Go in. Make for the Cinder Gate.
Turn back at the wall; this job is a grave.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=72 beats='He turns his back on the heat and walks the cinder line the other way; the crew will curse him and live, or curse him and not, and he will never know which; write the specific weight of a chosen refusal, the discipline of keeping a whole skin.'>>

choices.label: Keep walking. Let Ashmar burn without you.

choices.label: You carry the full haul: enough for the writ and the prize.
You carry a fair haul: enough to bargain hard.
You carry one thing of worth: a thin hand.

choices.label: Come down to the writ-block and see it through.

choices.label: Come down to the writ-block and see it through.

choices.label: Come down to the writ-block and see it through.

choices.label: Make for the Ember Road and the crew.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=78 beats='Arrival-neutral; the Ember Road runs the length of the burning wall, the wind rising, the crew a mile off at the cistern-lords' pens; write the walk arrival-agnostic, the heat and the choice coming; one thing above all will decide how this ends, whether the wardens are hunting him or not.'>>

choices.label: Come to the last stretch and read where you stand.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='Arrival-neutral flag gate; the instant of committing to the Ember Road, and the one question that governs everything after, whether Sef runs clean or runs hunted; write it arrival-agnostic and let the true state decide which single door opens.'>>

choices.label: You run clean: the wardens do not have your face.
You run hunted: the wardens are on the road behind you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='Arrival-neutral clean-branch goods gate; unhunted on the Ember Road, and everything now turns on what Sef carries for the crew and the crossing; write it arrival-agnostic and let the full haul, a fair haul, a thin hand, or empty hands pick the single road; exactly one applies.'>>

choices.label: Full haul: the writ, the prize, and weight to spare.
A fair haul: enough to get the crew out.
One thread of worth and little else.
Empty hands at the last.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=122 beats='The best of all the roads and it still has teeth; unhunted, full-handed, the writ in his coat and the crew in sight; but the last stretch of the Ember Road is a heat leg and the wall is failing; telegraph the danger plainly, the final crossing cannot be made on a dry throat, and everything now hangs on whether Sef kept any water back.'>>

choices.label: Face the last crossing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=88 beats='Arrival-neutral last-crossing water gate; the failing wall, the heat, the crew beyond; write it arrival-agnostic and let the water decide the single door, a crossing he can survive or one that kills him at the very end.'>>

choices.label: You kept water back: make the crossing.
You are bone-dry: the last crossing is a wall of heat.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=66 beats='Telegraphed to the last; a dry throat at a heat crossing is the death Sef was warned of from the first street; he makes it three steps into the shimmer; terse, grave.'>>

choices.label: The heat closes over him.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=death words=120 beats='He dies on the last crossing with the writ in his coat and the crew in sight, undone by a dry throat and not a warden's hand; write the death plain and hard, the cruelest arithmetic in Ashmar, everything won and no water left to carry it across; no gore, only the grey shimmer and the crew who watch a shape stop moving and never reach them.'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=118 beats='He makes the crossing with water to spare and comes out at the cistern-lords' pens with the writ and the crew before him; the one real triumph the city allows; but the wall is coming down behind and there is a last human choice in how he spends the moment he fought nine days for.'>>

choices.label: Face the crew and finish it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='The last decision, and an honest one; Sef holds the writ that frees the whole crew and the prize that would set him up for life; he can play it straight and get everyone out with the writ, he can spend the prize to buy out a straggler crew caught in the same pens, or he can push for one thing more and let greed choose for him; telegraph that the wall will not wait.'>>

choices.label: Free the crew with the writ and get everyone out clean.
Spend the prize to free the straggler crew too.
Push for one more haul before the wall comes down.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=60 beats='Greed at the last is the oldest death in Ashmar and he was warned of it; the wall does not wait for one more haul; terse, grave, and his own doing.'>>

choices.label: The wall comes down.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=132 beats='The clean escape, earned in full; unhunted, full-handed, water to spare, Sef lays the writ before the cistern-lords and walks the whole crew out of Ashmar as the wall comes down behind them; write it as the one complete win the burning city allows, the water-writ bought and the crew whole and the prize carried out beside them, hard-won and clean.'>>

<<FILL role=success words=122 beats='He spends the prize to free the straggler crew and walks two crews out with the writ and nothing over; write the quiet good of a man who traded his own future for strangers' lives, everyone out alive, the prize gone and not missed, a win with open hands.'>>

<<FILL role=death words=118 beats='He reaches for one more haul and the failing wall takes him and the reach together; write the death plain, a runner who had won and could not stop winning, the writ scattered in the ash and the crew left calling his name into a wall of fire; no gore, only the grey after and the cost of one step too many.'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=110 beats='A fair haul and a clean road; Sef reaches the pens with enough to bargain the writ but nothing over, and a hard choice between speed and thoroughness; telegraph that the wall is closing and a slow deal may not finish.'>>

choices.label: Buy the writ fast and run the crew out now.
Hold out for better terms as the wall closes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=60 beats='Holding out with the wall coming down is the gamble he was warned against; the cistern-lords do not blink and the fire does; terse.'>>

choices.label: The moment slips.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=118 beats='He buys the writ fast and runs the crew out through the closing lanes; write the win as real and lesser, the crew freed and out alive, the prize never his and not mourned, a clean job finished on a fair hand.'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=110 beats='He holds out a beat too long and the fire takes the choice from him; the crew get clear on the writ he half-bought, but Sef is cut off on the wrong side of the wall, alive and stranded in a burning city with nothing; write the sour draw, a win for the crew and a trap for the man.'>>

ending.title: The Wrong Side of the Wall

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=104 beats='One thread of worth and a crew priced past it; unhunted but nearly empty-handed, Sef must decide how far a single good will stretch; telegraph that it will not buy the whole crew and someone chooses who stays.'>>

choices.label: Buy out who you can and get them clear.
Gamble the one thread on the whole crew's freedom.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=58 beats='Gambling everything on a hand that cannot cover it is a fast way to lose it all; the cistern-lords take the thread and give nothing; terse.'>>

choices.label: The bluff falls flat.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=112 beats='He buys out the two he can and leaves the rest with a promise he may not keep; write the bitter arithmetic of a partial rescue, some of the crew walking free and some watching them go, a discovery about what one thread is worth and what it is not.'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=108 beats='He gambles the one thing he had and the cistern-lords keep it and the crew both; write the plain loss, a runner who reached too far with too little and came away with nothing, the crew still bonded and Sef with empty hands and a long walk out.'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='Empty-handed and clean; Sef reaches the pens with nothing to lay down and a crew he cannot buy free; telegraph that nerve alone does not open a cistern-lord's ledger, and the only choices left are bad ones.'>>

choices.label: Try to break the crew out by force and run.
Fall back and wait for a chance that may not come.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=58 beats='A forced break-out with empty hands against the cistern-lords' men is the worst odds in Ashmar; the pens are built for exactly this; terse, grave.'>>

choices.label: The pen-guards close.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=106 beats='The break-out fails in the first ten seconds and scatters the crew into a burning city; write the hard loss, no deaths he can be sure of and no rescue either, everyone flung loose into the fire on a runner's empty gamble.'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=104 beats='He falls back to wait for an opening and the wind turns first; write the quiet defeat, a runner who came all this way with nothing and could only watch the pens from the smoke, the crew's chance and his own burning down together while he waited.'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='Arrival-neutral burned-branch goods gate; hunted on the Ember Road with the wardens closing, and even now what Sef carries decides what he can salvage; write it arrival-agnostic and let a real haul, one thread, or empty hands pick the single road as the trap shuts; exactly one applies.'>>

choices.label: Hunted, but you carry weight to bargain with.
Hunted, with one thread to spend.
Hunted and empty-handed as the trap closes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=108 beats='Hunted but holding a real haul; the wardens are on the road and the crew is close, and the weight in Sef's coat is worth something only if he can spend it before they take him; telegraph that a hunted man cannot both bargain and run.'>>

choices.label: Spend the haul against the closing trap.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='The reckoning; being hunted has a price and it comes due now; buy the crew's freedom with the writ and let the wardens take you in the doing, throw it all down and run and hope the crew scatters clear, or hold the haul and force a stand on the pens; telegraph that none of these lets him keep both the haul and his freedom.'>>

choices.label: Buy the crew free with the writ; let them take you.
Throw it down and run; hope the crew scatters clear.
Hold the haul and force a stand at the pens.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=56 beats='A hunted man running the Ember Road with the wardens set for exactly this does not outrun the trap; terse, grave.'>>

choices.label: The road ends short.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=56 beats='A stand at the pens against warden numbers is the death he was warned of; terse, grave, and chosen.'>>

choices.label: They close from both ends.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=capture words=122 beats='He spends the writ to free the crew and the wardens fold him up in the same minute; write the costly good, the crew walking out on a writ paid for with Sef's own capture, a man who bought strangers' freedom with the last of his own and does not regret the arithmetic even in the branding-room.'>>

<<FILL role=death words=116 beats='He throws down the haul and runs and the wardens take the road out from under him; write the death plain, a hunted runner who gambled the last sprint and lost it, never knowing if the crew he scattered got clear; no gore, only the grey stop and the unanswered question.'>>

<<FILL role=death words=114 beats='He makes his stand at the pens and warden numbers end it the way numbers always do; write the death plain and without glory, a runner who chose the manner of it and nothing else, the crew's fate decided somewhere he cannot see.'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='Hunted with one thread; the wardens are closing and a single good is worth only what a bluff can make it worth, measured against how much of a run it could buy the crew.'>>

choices.label: Play the one thread against the trap.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=92 beats='Not enough and too late; bluff the thread and buy the crew a gap to scatter through, or break loud the other way and draw the wardens onto yourself; telegraph that both roads end with Sef in warden hands.'>>

choices.label: Bluff the thread; buy the crew a gap.
Break loud; draw them onto yourself.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=56 beats='The bluff half-works and the wardens turn on the obvious man; terse.'>>

choices.label: They come for you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=108 beats='The thread buys the crew a gap and Sef does not see whether they take it; write the hard, unresolved loss, everything spent to make a door for others and no way to know if anyone walked through it, the wardens closing as the smoke swallows the answer.'>>

<<FILL role=capture words=104 beats='He breaks loud and draws them and they take him at the road's end; write the capture plainly, a hunted runner who spent himself as a decoy and can only hope it counted, folded up while somewhere behind him the crew runs or does not.'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='Hunted and empty-handed as the trap shuts; Sef has announced himself and carries nothing to trade, and the Ember Road ahead is exactly the killing ground he was warned of; telegraph that there is no card left to play.'>>

choices.label: Meet the trap with nothing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=92 beats='Nothing between him and the closing dark; walk out and meet the wardens at the pens, try to warn the crew and run with them into a set that expected it, or give himself up first to buy them a breath; telegraph that only the manner of it is his to choose now.'>>

choices.label: Walk out to the pens and meet it.
Warn the crew and run with them.
Give yourself up first to buy them a breath.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=56 beats='He walks out with nothing and the trap takes him as it was built to; terse, grave.'>>

choices.label: The dark closes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=56 beats='He drags the crew into a doomed run and the ground that knew they were coming makes it brief; terse, grave.'>>

choices.label: The pens close on all of them.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=death words=116 beats='He walks the Ember Road into the trap and it ends the way he was warned it would; write the death plain and unadorned, a hunted, empty-handed runner meeting a set eleven days in the laying, the crew's fate sealed with his own; no gore, only the grey shut of it.'>>

<<FILL role=death words=114 beats='He warns the crew and runs and drags them into the same closing ground; write the death plain, a rescue that became a shared grave, Sef and the crew he came to save cut off together on a road that anticipated them; no gore, only the grey after.'>>

<<FILL role=capture words=110 beats='He gives himself up before they can take him and trades his own capture for a breath the crew might use; write the last small control of the burned and the blind, hands loose and open, buying a gap he will never know was taken; the wardens fold him up and the smoke keeps the rest.'>>
